name: Trial-00095 Exit Surface Diagnostic
edge_rationale: Trial-00095 already has a validated sweep/reclaim entry edge. This diagnostic tests whether the frozen baseline realized-R distribution is materially sensitive to simple winner or loser clipping, without changing entries, filters, governance, risk eligibility, or runtime behavior.
counterparty_or_market_mechanism: If sweep/reclaim winners often continue after first profit and losers invalidate quickly, a simple exit policy may improve realized R distribution. If improvements are narrow, fold-unstable, or driven by a few outliers, the current edge is exit-sensitive only in-sample.
frozen_assumptions: No production, PAPER, runtime, core, execution, orchestrator, or settings changes.
Entry population is frozen by replaying trial-00095 exact params.
No signal thresholds, entry filters, regime eligibility, governance, or risk gates may change.
Baseline control must be reported beside every diagnostic variant.
This V1 uses existing realized-R trade artifacts and is not a full intrabar exit replay.
Winner and loser clipping are diagnostic transformations, not executable exit policies.
MAE/MFE are diagnostics only and cannot approve a variant post hoc.
At most a hypothesis_for_future_validation verdict is allowed; no promotion-ready verdict.
expected_observation: A useful diagnostic would show that a broad, simple transformation improves paired-trade ER by at least 10%, preserves or improves PF, reduces max DD or tail loss, survives 2x/3x cost stress, and is not driven by one outlier trade.

kill_criteria.entry_count_mismatch: Any variant that changes the entry population is invalid.
kill_criteria.fold_instability: Improvement in fewer than 2 chronological folds is not decision-grade.
kill_criteria.sharp_surface: A single isolated winning variant without neighboring support is treated as overfit.
kill_criteria.cost_fragility: If improvement disappears at 2x costs, no action is allowed.
kill_criteria.methodology_bug: Any changed entries, data leakage, treating realized-R clipping as executable exit proof, or post-hoc threshold rescue invalidates the run.

failure_modes: Small sample makes exit optimum look stronger than it is.
A few extreme winners dominate the delta.
Intrabar TP/SL ordering determines the apparent edge.
Time stops improve win rate cosmetically while reducing expectancy.
Trailing variants smooth equity but cut the positive tail.
author: Codex
